post_rendered:
Rules
Credit to fdas/benjaminsz for template. Big thanks to GeneralCobra19 for help with flavor.
  • General Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not discuss which roles you submitted for the game or make any hints about what your picks were. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.9: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.10: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), pink (#FF00FF), purple (#9A00FF), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/code]
      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: Thornclaw9000
    • 2.2: Votes placed in hide tags or quotes will be ignored.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day strictly more than 1/2 of the votes, rounded down, are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day greater than 1/2 of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
    • 2.13: Bolded votes are allowed if and only if you have played in a game prior to Mafia Game 165.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 168 hours (7 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time.
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 168 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 48 hours (2 days) after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator and co-moderator do not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the moderator or co-moderator makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator and co-moderator find a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives too many PM Prods (~5, depending on case), he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.
    • 4.6: Refrain from angleshooting, especially about flavor. (I am very lazy)
  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I, Thornclaw9000, am the moderator of Mafia Game.
    • 5.2: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • 5.3: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.4: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.5: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Setup
    This game will use the Bastard++ setup. This is a semi-open setup. Please look at the link for more information about the setup, but note that some of the role names may have been altered.
    • There are 13 players.
    • This setup uses a Daystart.
    • Rolecops/Stalkers are guaranteed to be sane.
    • Doctors prevent one nightkill.
    • Nightkills are indistinguishable. There is no difference between a Mafia, Serial Killer, or Vigilante kill.
    • Normal hang majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of all living players, Cult win majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of all living players.
    • The role modifier "Hated" means that 1 less vote is required for hang majority.
    • The role modifier "Loved" is the opposite of "Hated", meaning that one more vote is required for hang majority.
    • The role modifier "Bulletproof" prevents 1 night-kill per night.
    • The role modifier "Weak" means that if the person targets a mafia for their night-action, they die.
    • If the role modifier "1-Shot" is added, depending on what is connected to it allows an ability to be used only once.
    • The moderator will not purposefully present incorrect information, and role modifier(s) (if any) will show in Role PMs and on flip. The only exception to this is the “Hidden Condemnee” role modifier, which will not show in Role PMs, but will show on flip.
    • This rule is applicable to only Jester, Survivor, and Mafia Goon. Actual roles may have different flavor names than the examples in the Role PMs tab. Example flavornames are not necessarily used, even if the role has one or two instances in the game. Additionally, there may be multiple instances of the same flavorname for these roles.
    • Each player will have a personal Vanilla Townie flavorname and flavor, regardless of actual role.
    • If the randomization of the setup results in an impossible setup, then it will be reranded. Examples include but are not limited to "CCCCCCJTSK" (no corresponding result for 6 Cs) and "CJJJSSSSK" (1+3+5+3+1+3 = 16 unique roles which is greater than the number of players in the setup, 13)
  • Role PMs
    Town Rolecop wrote:
    You are Aya Shameimaru (Town Rolecop). How exciting! You have many ideas for the Bunbunmaru Newspaper - too bad no one treats your newspaper seriously, it's not like you exaggerate or make up anything!

    Each night, you may listen to the wind around a certain player to get their general demeanor.

    You win when all opposing factions are dead.

    Investigation Results
    • Cult: Cult Leader, Cultist
    • Self-Aligned: Jester, Survivor, Condemner, Serial Killer
    • Mafia: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker, Mafia Stalker
    • Vanilla Townie: Vanilla Townie, Investigation Immune Serial Killer
    • Doctor: Doctor
    • Vigilante: Vigilante
    • Rolecop: Town Rolecop


    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • 1-Shot: You can only rolecop once in this game.
    Doctor wrote:
    You are Eirin Yagokoro (Doctor). As the premier doctor of Gensokyo, your duty hasn't changed at all with this incident. Your job might be tough, but you'll try to prevent as many fatalities and injuries as you can until this is all resolved.

    Each night you may protect one player from one nightkill with your incredible medical knowledge.

    You win when all opposing factions are dead.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • 1-Shot: You can only protect once in this game.
    1-Shot Vigilante wrote:
    You are Marisa Kirasame (1-Shot Vigilante). Another incident-ze? You’re pretty sure either you, Reimu, or Sanae will figure it out-ze.

    You do know you have a trump card up your sleeve, in case any of the youkai decide to make any nefarious moves-ze, which is that once per game you may nightkill a player with your Master Spark-ze!

    You win when all opposing factions are dead-ze.
    Vanilla Townie wrote:
    You are <insert touhou character> (Vanilla Townie). <insert flavortext>

    VT Flavornames
    Alice Margatroid
    Byakuren Hijiri
    Kasen Ibaraki
    Momiji Inubashiri
    Nitori Kawashiro
    Nue Houjuu
    Reisen Udongein Inaba
    Suika Ibuki
    Sumireko Usami
    Suwako Moriya
    Tenshi Hinanawi
    Youmu Konpaku
    Yuyuko Saigyouji


    You win when all opposing factions are dead.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • Hated: For hang majority calculations, 1 is added to your votecount.
    • Loved: For hang majority calculations, 1 is subtracted from your votecount.
    Cult Leader wrote:
    You are Cirno Fumo (Cult Leader). As an ultimate being of chaos, you seek to take over Gensokyo during this tumultuous time.

    Each night you may convert one player to a Fumo (Cultist).

    You win if the number of cult-aligned players alive is strictly greater than the number of other players alive.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • Hated and Weak: For hang majority calculations, 1 is added to your votecount. You also die if you target a member of the Mafia.
    • Weak: You die if you target a member of the Mafia.
    • Loved: For hang majority calculations, 1 is subtracted from your votecount.
    Cultist wrote:
    You are a Fumo (Cultist). Being a marketable plushie is fine.

    You win if the number of cult-aligned players alive is strictly greater than the number of other players alive.
    Jester wrote:
    You are Kaguya Houraisan (Jester). You're quite curious as to what is in that massive gap and want to investigate! You're bored with your eternal life, so you are completely fine with dying as a result.

    You win if you are hung. Your win condition does not end the game.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • Loved: For hang majority calculations, 1 is subtracted from your votecount.
    • 1-Shot Bulletproof: You will survive one nightkill.
    Condemner wrote:
    You are Chen (Condemner). Your master's master is dead. You want the culprit to be held responsible, and you've found some clues and leads that indicate <random player> being Yukari's murderer. Clearly those who think it's the entirety of the Scarlet Devil Mansion are crazy. Justice must be served!

    You win if <random player> is hung while you are alive. Your win condition does not end the game. You will leave the game if <random player> dies in any way while you are alive.
    Survivor wrote:
    You are Reimu Hakurei (Survivor). Yukari is probably pulling one of her shenanigans again, although the spell card rule being gone is slightly concerning. You'll try to hold down the fort and survive until Yukari is satisfied and stops fooling around.

    You win when any faction wins while you are alive.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • Hated: For hang majority calculations, 1 is added to your votecount.
    Serial Killer wrote:
    You are Utsuho Reiuji (Serial Killer). You aren't quite sure what's going on, something about Yukari? You do remember that someone told you to take over Gensokyo... or something like that? Now seems like a perfect time!

    Each night you may kill one player with the power of nuclear fusion.

    You win when you are the sole player alive.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • Hated: For hang majority calculations, 1 is added to your votecount.
    • 1-Shot Bulletproof: You will survive one nightkill.
    • Investigation Immune: You turn up as “Vanilla Townie” when investigated.
    • Investigation Immune + 1-Shot Bulletproof: You turn up as “Vanilla Townie” when investigated and you will survive one nightkill.
    Mafia Goon wrote:
    You are Sakuya Izayoi (Mafia Goon). The youkai seem to be blaming your mistress, Remilia, for Yukari's death. As her maid, you're going to protect her as well as everyone else living at the Scarlet Devil Mansion until this incident is solved.

    Your partner(s) is/are <partner(s)>. Each night your faction may kill one player.

    You win when the number of mafia-aligned players alive is equal to or greater than the number of other players alive.
    Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
    You are Remilia Scarlet (Mafia Roleblocker). You're scared... you know you didn't do anything, at least this time. The other youkai seem to want to kill you as well as the other Scarlet Devil Mansion residents... eek!

    Your partner(s) is/are <partner(s)>. Each night your faction may kill one player. Each night you may also roleblock one player by manipulating their fate, making their action (if any) ineffective. You may perform your factional kill at the same time as your roleblock.

    You win when the number of mafia-aligned players alive is equal to or greater than the number of other players alive.

    Potential Role Modifiers:
    • 1-Shot: You can only roleblock once this game.
    Mafia Stalker wrote:
    You are Patchouli Knowledge (Mafia Stalker). As a shut-in, you only learnt of Yukari's death when the other Scarlet Devil Mansion residents came back in shock. Remilia is asking for your help to protect her, and you obliged.

    Your partner(s) is/are <partner(s)>. Each night your faction may kill one player. Each night you may also stalk one player to get clues about their general identity, and you'll try to look them up in your library. You may perform your factional kill at the same time as your stalk.

    You win when the number of mafia-aligned players alive is equal to or greater than the number of other players alive.

    Investigation Results
    • Cult: Cult Leader, Cultist
    • Self-Aligned: Jester, Survivor, Condemner, Serial Killer
    • Mafia: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker, Mafia Stalker
    • Vanilla Townie: Vanilla Townie, Investigation Immune Serial Killer
    • Doctor: Doctor
    • Vigilante: Vigilante
    • Rolecop: Town Rolecop


    Potential Role Modifier:
    • 1-Shot: You can only investigate once this game.

  • Notable Rules
    • Hang majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of alive players. Note that this is different than the original setup.
    • No hang majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of alive players.
    • Town can only win if there are no SKs, no Mafia members, no Cult members, and no Condemners alive.
    • Survivor cannot win with SK.
    • Both mafia and cult can win regardless of the composition of the other players.
    • Jester and Condemner do not end the game if they win. Condemner will leave the game once their condemnee has died in any way.
    • Refrain from angleshooting, especially about flavor.
    • Note that the Role PMs tab lacks flavor due to laziness. Each player will have a personal Vanilla Townie flavorname and I can create flavor for it if requested. (i am very lazy!) I might end up writing flavor for them anyways throughout the game if I have the time.
    • Mafia and Cult have daytime and nighttime access to private factional chats.

  • Confirm Rule Quiz
    PM the answers to these question to me to confirm. Refrain from giving the answers away here. You must pass this test with a score of at least 7/8 to pass. If you do not pass, see me after class (I will give you the answers), but you might be publicly shamed. Hint: Just read the notable rules tab for questions 1-4.
    1A/B/C. How many player votes do you need to hang a normal player when there are 13 players alive? What about a hated player? A loved player? (3 points)
    2. How many player votes do you need to no hang when there are 13 players alive? (1 point)
    3A. Can the mafia faction win while the SK or the cult faction is still alive? (1 point)
    3B. Can the cult faction win while the SK or the mafia faction is still alive? (1 point)
    4. Can a Survivor win together with the SK? (1 point)
    5. Can town win together with the mafia faction? If so, how? (1 point)
Alive (3)
++SFGiantsFan, Aya Shameimaru (Town Rolecop)
math_turtle37, Suika Ibuki (Hated Vanilla Townie)
scannose, Youmu Konpaku (Loved Vanilla Townie)

Scipio 1, Cirno Fumo (Loved Cult Leader)

fdas, Remilia Scarlet (1-Shot Mafia Roleblocker)
candythecorgi, Hong Meiling (Mafia Goon)
jeremylu, Kaguya Houraisan (Loved Jester)
Fvr_Vain, Marisa Kirasame (1-Shot Vigilante, Hidden Condemnee)

Not Dead (2)

End of Game, Town + Condemner + Loved Jester win, Post #1258

Story
Warning: I am not good at writing.
Gensokyo hadn't had an incident in almost a month, when Yukari Yakumo suddenly went missing. While this wouldn't normally be of concern, a massive gap appearing, the Great Hakurei Barrier weakening, and the spell card rules vanishing have led to great concern. The massive gap mysteriously had text spelled out - "Yukari Yakumo". Reimu threw a rock in to investigate, and the text suddenly changed to spell "Rock". The shrine maidens, people, and youkai quickly realized that Yukari is very likely dead, whoever killed Yukari was a threat, and the power balance and lives of Gensokyo may be in danger. Every clue was pointing to the Scarlet Devil Mansion being behind all of this.

Thornclaw9000 (Yukari Yakumo) is dead. Day 1 begins.

[*] [b]5.5:[/b] The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. [/list]

[*][hide="Setup"]
This game will use the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Bastard%2B%2B]Bastard++[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup. Please look at the link for more information about the setup, but note that some of the role names may have been altered.
[list]
[*] There are 13 players.
[*] This setup uses a Daystart.
[*] Rolecops/Stalkers are guaranteed to be sane.
[*] Doctors prevent one nightkill.
[*] Nightkills are indistinguishable. There is no difference between a Mafia, Serial Killer, or Vigilante kill.
[*] Normal hang majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of all living players, Cult win majority is strictly greater than 1/2 of all living players.
[*] The role modifier "Hated" means that 1 less vote is required for hang majority.
[*] The role modifier "Loved" is the opposite of "Hated", meaning that one more vote is required for hang majority.
[*] The role modifier "Bulletproof" prevents 1 night-kill per night.
[*] The role modifier "Weak" means that if the person targets a mafia for their night-action, they die.
[*] If the role modifier "1-Shot" is added, depending on what is connected to it allows an ability to be used only once.
[*] The moderator will not purposefully present incorrect information, and role modifier(s) (if any) will show in Role PMs and on flip. The only exception to this is the “Hidden Condemnee” role modifier, which will not show in Role PMs, but will show on flip.
[*] This rule is applicable to only Jester, Survivor, and Mafia Goon. Actual roles may have different flavor names than the examples in the Role PMs tab. Example flavornames are not necessarily used, even if the role has one or two instances in the game. Additionally, there may be multiple instances of the same flavorname for these roles.
[*] Each player will have a personal Vanilla Townie flavorname and flavor, regardless of actual role.
[*] If the randomization of the setup results in an impossible setup, then it will be reranded. Examples include but are not limited to "CCCCCCJTSK" (no corresponding result for 6 Cs) and "CJJJSSSSK" (1+3+5+3+1+3 = 16 unique roles which is greater than the number of players in the setup, 13)
[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Story"]
[b][u]Warning: I am not good at writing. [/u][/b]
Gensokyo hadn't had an incident in almost a month, when Yukari Yakumo suddenly went missing. While this wouldn't normally be of concern, a massive gap appearing, the Great Hakurei Barrier weakening, and the spell card rules vanishing have led to great concern. The massive gap mysteriously had text spelled out -  "Yukari Yakumo". Reimu threw a rock in to investigate, and the text suddenly changed to spell "Rock". The shrine maidens, people, and youkai quickly realized that Yukari is very likely dead, whoever killed Yukari was a threat, and the power balance and lives of Gensokyo may be in danger. Every clue was pointing to the Scarlet Devil Mansion being behind all of this.

[color=#9a00ff]Thornclaw9000 (Yukari Yakumo)[/color] is dead. Day 1 begins.
[/hide]

basic setup info :D
Randomization
The moderator randomly chooses 9 numbers from 1-100, repeating permitted. Then, each number is turned into a letter as follows:

1-30 = M (Mafia)
31-44 = C (Cult)
45-58 = J (Jester)
59-72 = T (Town)
73-86 = S (Survival)
87-100 = K (Killers)
The mod then notes how many of each letter is generated.

Setup Determination
Powerroles are inserted into the setup depending on how many of each letter are generated. For example, when looking at the "C" list, look at how many Cs you received. If you get no Cs, do not add any roles from the C list (exception: "M" list has a set of roles to be added if 0 Ts are generated). If you get three Cs, look at where it says CCC and add the corresponding powerroles into the game. In this case, add a Cult Leader.

Cult Roles
C = Hated Weak Cult-Leader

CC = Weak Cult-Leader

CCC = Cult-Leader

CCCC = Loved Cult-Leader

CCCCC = Loved Cult Leader + Cultist

Jester Roles
J = Loved Jester

JJ = Jester

JJJ = 2 Jesters

JJJJ = 1-Shot-Bullet-Proof Jester + Jester

JJJJJ = 2 1-Shot-Bullet-Proof Jesters

Town Roles
TTTT = 1-Shot Inspector + Hated Towny

TTT = Inspector + Loved Towny

TT = Inspector + 1-Shot Doctor + Hated Towny

T = Inspector + Doctor + Loved Towny

0 T's = Inspector + Doctor + 1-Shot Vigilante + Hated Towny + Loved Towny

Survival Roles
S = Condemner + Hidden Condemnee

SS = Condemner + Hidden Condemnee + Hated Survivor

SSS = Condemner + Hidden Condemnee + Survivor

SSSS = Condemner + Hidden Condemnee + Survivor + Hated Survivor

SSSSS = Condemner + Hidden Condemnee + 2 Survivors

Killer Roles
K = Hated Serial Killer

KK = Killer

KKK = 1-Shot-Bullet-Proof Serial Killer

KKKK = Inspection Immune Serial Killer

KKKKK = Inspection Immune 1-Shot-Bullet-Proof Serial Killer

Mafia Roles
MMMMMM= 2 Goons

MMMMM = 1-Shot Roleblocker + Goon

MMMM = Roleblocker + Goon

MMM = 1-Shot Stalker + 2 goons

MM = Stalker + 2 Goons

M = Stalker + 1-Shot Roleblocker + Goon

0 M's = Stalker + Roleblocker + Goon

these are my opinions regarding the whole vain/jeremy claim incident, and only my opinions so please correct me if you disagree with anything i say

vain softed 1-shot vigilante and jeremy softed an unknown power role before vain hardclaimed; from what i see, jeremy likely countersofted only because he misinterpreted vain's soft. it is unlikely that either vain or jeremy is fakeclaiming - especially when any of the antitowns even determine what the town power roles even are.

there is a possibility that either vain or jeremy (or even both) are fakeclaiming, but it's not worth it to hang either of them day 1, especially when we have many other tools to determine the validity of claims such as a rolecop if that ends up being absolutely necessary.

note that even if we are explicitly told in one way or another that one of jeremy/vain is a vig and the other is a scum, vain's claim is not guaranteed to be true, since we can not differentiate whether vain or jeremy performed the vigilante shot; both of them can claim to have done so.

@aayr i don't think vain's behavior is executioner-like, as he is only pushing jeremy because jeremy countersofted him. even if he is, i think executioner is a role that should be townsiding (most of the time).

This is for future reference which we can use as soft evidence later if/when vain flips

Anyway I was going to analyze vain further but recent developments take precedence here. Forgive me for the summary, but writing it out helps ground my thoughts.

Vain is claiming 1-Shot Vigilante. According to the possible town setups, there is only one scenario which generates a 1-Shot Vigilante, and we also have four other PRs if Vain is to be believed. Now, according to the Town should never lie principle, I would suggest that if any town has a PR role that directly contradicts this (namely, 1-Shot Inspector, 1-Shot Doctor, or themselves 1-Shot Vig), they should claim that they have a contradictory role, but NOT what they are. This puts town in a situation where one of two people are definitely antitown. Granted, one can be jester, but we'll cross that bridge if someone cc's anyway.

That said, I actually suggest that we don't do this (that is, don't soft-counterclaim even if you are able to). Why not? Bastard++ generates a lot of non-town-aligned roles. If Vain is to be believed, we have 0T, which implies that there is a much higher probability of a cult, powerful mafia, killer, jester, survivor, etc all existing. If we have a cult leader, three mafia, one killer, one jester, and one condemner (this looks somewhat likely assuming 0T), this is 7 non-town-aligned roles existing, that is, people that are not necessarily inclined to vote in the town's best interest. Do you know what is? 6.5 (!). This means THERE IS A REAL POSSIBILITY THAT OVER HALF OF US DO NOT HAVE TOWN'S INTEREST IN MIND. That means going one-for-one (killing both Vain + counterclaimer) is BAD. Even if you take one out (also somewhat likely) and we have only 6 non-town, going one-for-one is only barely better. I think town needs to aspire to better hangs than fifty-fifty shots for this reason. If someone does have a role that contradicts Vain, I am thus of the opinion that they should just (outwardly) suspect them.

Call this setup-meta-gaming but this is my real opinion.

Now, let me explore my opinion on whether Fvr_Vain's claim is trustworthy or not.

I don't think Fvr_Vain anti-town does this. Why? The wagon analysis above (I think) shows that they are not really in danger of being hanged. Claiming is a big risk as I think the first instinct whenever scum tries to pr claim is to worry about counterclaims. Why do it unnecessarily? Even if they perceived themselves to be in danger, why 1-Shot Vig? This makes other roles much more likely to target them (e.g. cult, killer, again, a very likely option, although less likely if 1-Shot Vig is fake as in this example). Seems like something like 1-Shot Doctor would be safer from this perspective. I don't even think it's WIFOM in this case; what do the other roles have to lose by not targeting them? Perhaps anti-town Fvr_Vain does this if they know there are few M's or K's or C's or whatever (and thus more likely to be T's, thus less likely 1-Shot Vig is directly counterclaimable). But all of this aside, my primary conclusion is I don't think there's enough pressure for anti-town to make a fakeclaim play that is generally just for emergencies (i.e. in imminent danger of being hung).

Also, the fact that no one has soft-CC'd yet seems to support this (again, I don't think we should, but I don't think this is most people's first instinct). So, I will give the benefit of the doubt (at least for now) to Vain. With that in mind, I will FoS ming , and to a lesser extent aayr and candy as a possible condemner on Vain. (Correct me if I'm wrong, but PR roles can be condemnees, right?)

pandabear10 wrote:
Vain is claiming 1-Shot Vigilante. According to the possible town setups, there is only one scenario which generates a 1-Shot Vigilante, and we also have four other PRs if Vain is to be believed. Now, according to the Town should never lie principle, I would suggest that if any town has a PR role that directly contradicts this (namely, 1-Shot Inspector, 1-Shot Doctor, or themselves 1-Shot Vig), they should claim that they have a contradictory role, but NOT what they are. This puts town in a situation where one of two people are definitely antitown. Granted, one can be jester, but we'll cross that bridge if someone cc's anyway.

That said, I actually suggest that we don't do this (that is, don't soft-counterclaim even if you are able to). Why not? Bastard++ generates a lot of non-town-aligned roles. If Vain is to be believed, we have 0T, which implies that there is a much higher probability of a cult, powerful mafia, killer, jester, survivor, etc all existing. If we have a cult leader, three mafia, one killer, one jester, and one condemner (this looks somewhat likely assuming 0T), this is 7 non-town-aligned roles existing, that is, people that are not necessarily inclined to vote in the town's best interest. Do you know what is? 6.5 (!). This means THERE IS A REAL POSSIBILITY THAT OVER HALF OF US DO NOT HAVE TOWN'S INTEREST IN MIND. That means going one-for-one (killing both Vain + counterclaimer) is BAD. Even if you take one out (also somewhat likely) and we have only 6 non-town, going one-for-one is only barely better. I think town needs to aspire to better hangs than fifty-fifty shots for this reason. If someone does have a role that contradicts Vain, I am thus of the opinion that they should just (outwardly) suspect them.

post_canonical:
[quote=pandabear10]Vain is claiming 1-Shot Vigilante. According to the possible town setups, there is only one scenario which generates a 1-Shot Vigilante, and we also have four other PRs if Vain is to be believed. Now, according to the Town should never lie principle, I would suggest that if any town has a PR role that directly contradicts this (namely, 1-Shot Inspector, 1-Shot Doctor, or themselves 1-Shot Vig), they should claim that they have a contradictory role, but NOT what they are. This puts town in a situation where one of two people are definitely antitown. Granted, one can be jester, but we'll cross that bridge if someone cc's anyway.

That said, I actually suggest that we don't do this (that is, don't soft-counterclaim even if you are able to). Why not? Bastard++ generates a lot of non-town-aligned roles. If Vain is to be believed, we have 0T, which implies that there is a much higher probability of a cult, powerful mafia, killer, jester, survivor, etc all existing. If we have a cult leader, three mafia, one killer, one jester, and one condemner (this looks somewhat likely assuming 0T), this is 7 non-town-aligned roles existing, that is, people that are not necessarily inclined to vote in the town's best interest. Do you know what $\frac{13}{2}$ is? 6.5 (!). This means THERE IS A REAL POSSIBILITY THAT OVER HALF OF US DO NOT HAVE TOWN'S INTEREST IN MIND. That means going one-for-one (killing both Vain + counterclaimer) is BAD. Even if you take one out (also somewhat likely) and we have only 6 non-town, going one-for-one is only barely better. I think town needs to aspire to better hangs than fifty-fifty shots for this reason. If someone does have a role that contradicts Vain, I am thus of the opinion that they should just (outwardly) suspect them.[/quote]

post_rendered:
I think I have a very good reason to hate panda's #657 as well as most of the other wall posts.

There are quite a few issues with panda's overall posts during this entire game day. There are also a few mechanical reasons why panda actually have very excellent cult/sk/wolf equity.

I think panda doesn't actually realize that half the players have already softlocked me town due to various mechanical and statistical reasons and there isnt actually anything he or ming can do about it.

The over-analysis on mechanical interactions that are less than plausible are taking up way too much space in his word count and post count. Given that there are likely to be >/= 5 players who are killer or converter roles, there are very little reasons why town will spend almost half their analysis on the possibility of non-interactive 3p roles.

given the wise words of a person who studied stats:
Quote:
please consider whats probable not what's possible.

It strikes me as extremely unlikely that as town player that panda has chosen not to directly mentioning killer / converter roles at all in this entire day given his previously known mechanical prowess.

Rather, most of panda's analysis have been revolving around interactions that to me seem rather unimportant.

He is also not in the list of the 4-5 players that I mechanically very much have cleared. putting him in the 8 remaining players that are likely to contain at around 1-3 vt and 5-7 3p/wolf roles.

He has also deliberately avoided giving precise reads to (likely intention) avoid creating attention drawing wagons or towntrains.

That said I would like the following players to evaluate the feasibility of a panda cfg at eod, and thoughts on panda's possible alignment based on observation and mechanical information from ur pov.

fdas
sfgf
jeremy
candy
turtle
scan
scipio
ad

post_rendered:
I guess aayr hang is fine. I dont really want to hang exec or surv because it is actually counterproductive.

but scannose claim is absolutely awful you are in my list of 5 out of poe players.

if you were a doc cc it would be even worse.

Here's a touch of night strategy with the remaining town players:

I believe cop in this setup has an ability that is not as valuable as 1svig because by day two its a mechanical race to eliminate scum where investigation doesn't carry as much weight, because we will be basing hangs on counterclaims anyway. There are likely to be cases where triple claims.

That said: in this game cop should be seeking to find greenchecks instead of redchecks as the game will go down to poe instead of scumhunting.

I am still more convinced that a doc protect should go on vig as the vig shot is almost a guarenteed force kill to slow down the rate at which anti-town roles reach parity (which is an alarming rate given an average of around 7.2 town players at start of day 1 given this setup). It is worth protecting vig until the vig confirms that his shot actually goes through because a roleblocker may be present.

Here are the scenarios of roleblocking interactions:

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 1 nk go on vig:
antitown wastes 1 nk, vig shot does not go through, game continues,
(overall 1 nk wasted for antitown)

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 2 nk go on vig (both sk and wolf party)
antitown wastes 1 nk, vig shot does not go through and dies with vig,
(overall 1 nk wasted for antitown and 1 nk wasted for town, 0 kills wasted overall)

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 0 nk go on vig
vig kill does not go through and is kept.
(overall 0 kills wasted in favor of town)

Here are the scenarios when doc does not protect vig:

- 1 nk go on vig, roleblock go on vig.
vig dies, kill is buried
(overall 1 kill lost for town)

- 2 nk go on vig, roleblock go on vig
vig dies, kill is buried, 1 kill wasted on both sides
(overall 0 kill lost for town)

In order to prevent the game from reaching town non-majority, it is actually better interest to protect vig instead of cop in this setup as it minimizes the amount of kills lost for town, which is much more valuable given by day 2 the alignment of almost all players will be known.

post_rendered:
Fvr_Vain wrote:
I guess aayr hang is fine. I dont really want to hang exec or surv because it is actually counterproductive.

but scannose claim is absolutely awful you are in my list of 5 out of poe players.

if you were a doc cc it would be even worse.

Here's a touch of night strategy with the remaining town players:

I believe cop in this setup has an ability that is not as valuable as 1svig because by day two its a mechanical race to eliminate scum where investigation doesn't carry as much weight, because we will be basing hangs on counterclaims anyway. There are likely to be cases where triple claims.

That said: in this game cop should be seeking to find greenchecks instead of redchecks as the game will go down to poe instead of scumhunting.

I am still more convinced that a doc protect should go on vig as the vig shot is almost a guarenteed force kill to slow down the rate at which anti-town roles reach parity (which is an alarming rate given an average of around 7.2 town players at start of day 1 given this setup). It is worth protecting vig until the vig confirms that his shot actually goes through because a roleblocker may be present.

Here are the scenarios of roleblocking interactions:

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 1 nk go on vig:
antitown wastes 1 nk, vig shot does not go through, game continues,
(overall 1 nk wasted for antitown)

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 2 nk go on vig (both sk and wolf party)
antitown wastes 1 nk, vig shot does not go through and dies with vig,
(overall 1 nk wasted for antitown and 1 nk wasted for town, 0 kills wasted overall)

- doc protects vig, roleblocker blocks vig, 0 nk go on vig
vig kill does not go through and is kept.
(overall 0 kills wasted in favor of town)

Here are the scenarios when doc does not protect vig:

- 1 nk go on vig, roleblock go on vig.
vig dies, kill is buried
(overall 1 kill lost for town)

- 2 nk go on vig, roleblock go on vig
vig dies, kill is buried, 1 kill wasted on both sides
(overall 0 kill lost for town)

In order to prevent the game from reaching town non-majority, it is actually better interest to protect vig instead of cop in this setup as it minimizes the amount of kills lost for town, which is much more valuable given by day 2 the alignment of almost all players will be known.

here's the somewhat tldr for the rest of town to read.

but it would be a good option to hammer now to prevent excessive wifom which always favor scum.

Vote: aayr

@scan do the honors to your cc

post_rendered:
Too many people are asking about how night actions work in this game so uh here
Natural Action Resolution is used in this game. This means if two actions conflict with each other, they will be processed with the following priority. If there are no conflicts between two actions, they will both occur. Note that is simplified to just be about the possible interactions in this setup. Note that 1-shot abilities will not be consumed if they are prevented from occurring.

1. Block
2. Protect (can conflict with block)
3. Kill (can conflict with block, protect*)
4. Recruit (can conflict with block, kill**)
5. Inspect (can conflict with block, recruit***)

*Conflicts only if protect and kill successfully target same player after resolving conflicting action(s) above and all non-conflicting action(s), if any. 1 protect will prevent 1 kill.
**Conflicts only if kill and recruit successfully target same player after resolving conflicting action(s) above and all non-conflicting action(s), if any. Recruit will be unsuccessful and treated as not happening.
***Conflicts only if recruit and inspect successfully target same player after resolving conflicting action(s) above and all non-conflicting action(s), if any. Inspect will return "Cult".

ig you can go ahead and verify me, unless you're worried im the jester now
sigh
initially when i saw jeremy's claim, i figured out he was one of three things
- the cop
- the loved jester (i later realized he could be any jester)
- the loved cult leader

i quickly crossed out the last option because it 1. had a less than 5% chance to occur mechwise 2. requires jeremy to somehow think that his role implies the existence/nonexistence of a loved townie, and i had no reason to counterclaim in any of the two more likely scenarios
eventually i also ignored the second option since jeremy seemed contributive enough, turns out he probably just wanted people to believe in his claim

jeremy did notably asked for verification a few times, but he didn't push it and i assumed he just wanted to make his claim more believable

i wasn't certain aayr was scum when he claimed - especially since i was townleaning him, but there were a couple of reasons that made me change my mind
- he presented his claim in an awkward way
- he seemed confused on mech but not being active in solving his confusion (which i stated in thread)
- he voted vain, the uncced vig while he
- most importantly, i still thought jeremy was the softed cop lol

i thought jeremy was signaling to me after aayr's claim lol
like jeremy went straight from "i agree that real cop shouldn't out" to "can we have a 1 for 1 ty" in 6 hours or so
jeremy also didn't like aayr's claim

my fakeclaim was also partially a reaction test
if aayr was the real cop (or if he had significant chance in being one), i would retract my claim and counterclaim jeremy instead, which would be helpful because it reveals that there is a jester in the setup who is not chris
and i could do it safely, at no cost of outing any other unknown claims

though i didn't do that because aayr logged off happily 5 minutes after i counterclaimed, which was just obvwolf

i only started to have suspicion on jeremy at the start of day 2
i intentionally hid my online status (lmao) so that i could safely take the check jeremy was softing and pretend it was my own, but he wasn't softing anything (the only thing which looked like a potential check was his scipio read, but he was not near clear enough in saying it was a soft)
then there was a sfgf claim, which i first ignored because his claim looked fake enough but sigh (and also that we were thinking he was a hated townie / fdas claimed hated townie right before him)

though there was still no reason for me to claim until jeremy led a wagon on himself

now that all of these have happened, i think the solve is {fdas, chris} and all other claims are legit

This is a second post written at night with no knowledge of n1 flips. (help i have too much time) The previous post claims that the remaining scum are ming and corgi, with a wing possibility of scipio. Go read that if you haven't. The goal of this post is mostly about SK and cult, although I will give mention to other roles, town's priority should probably be cult leader either today or tomorrow; leaving cult unaddressed is super dangerous, while fundamentally sk is always alone. (and just in case I need to say it, we absolutely cannot worry about jester/condemner today) I talk more about viable hangs later in this post.

It should also be pointed out that there's an outside chance that sk or cult or both don't exist but given we seem to have 0t, it's much more likely that they exist (even more so if vain's shot gets rb'd, as that implies 0m/1m).

Mech wise, assuming my mafread is right, we have doc/hated town in {chris,fdas,turtle,ad333,sfgf,scip,me}, as well as (likely) sk+cult leader, condemner, jester. That's six likely roles in seven people, and we may also have a survivor with decent likelihood as well. Keep in mind likely one person is converted already (not necessarily in this list) and there is the outside possibility that there are two cultists now.

I'm looking at the following criteria when searching for sk/cult leader. most of these are mafia tells too, fortunately if someone is acting mafia and not in the list above, they're probably sk or cult!
- scumhunting is a null tell against sk/cult, as sk is just as interested in eliminating competing third parties as town. thus I'm ignoring however contributive someone has appeared thus far/cult. That said, NOT scumhunting is a tell.
- sk/cult leader is not interested in drawing attention; fundamentally sk has no backup, and cult leader in d1 is more or less serial killer. this may involve backing down, or have an unwillingness to follow up their reads
- interested in voting anyone, particularly through bandwagoning & none of their own reasons.
- particularly to sk, a particular interest in scum/town reads, as cult doesn't want to convert someone that's gonna get hung/nkd

as d2 progresses we'll be able to search for more stuff that might distinguish sk/cult from each other, but as i'm taking the time during night to be more thorough in my thoughts and stuff when I can have guarantee nothing else will interrupt me, I have d1 to work with.

I'm ignoring the confirmed towns as well as ming/corgi here.

I'm giving a summary of my preanalysis thoughts, then an analysis, then post-analysis thoughts.

The people that made the least impression on me are turtle, chris, and fdas. Turtle, obviously, but it's literally impossible to have any productive discussion about him. Chris's posts have been mostly fluff; and the few reads he gave were ones that were with no explanation.

Chris

(and just in case I need to say it, we absolutely cannot worry about jester/condemner today) I talk more about viable hangs later in this post.

It should also be pointed out that there's an outside chance that sk or cult or both don't exist but given we seem to have 0t, it's much more likely that they exist (even more so if vain's shot gets rb'd, as that implies 0m/1m).

[i]That's six likely roles in seven people,[/i] and we may also have a survivor with decent likelihood as well. I'm looking at the following criteria when searching for sk/cult leader. most of these are mafia tells too, fortunately if someone is acting mafia and not in the list above, they're probably sk or cult!
- scumhunting is a null tell against sk/cult, as sk is just as interested in eliminating competing third parties as town. thus I'm ignoring however contributive someone has appeared thus far/cult. That said, NOT scumhunting is a tell.
- sk/cult leader is not interested in drawing attention; fundamentally sk has no backup, and cult leader in d1 is more or less serial killer. this may involve backing down, or have an unwillingness to follow up their reads
- interested in voting anyone, particularly through bandwagoning & none of their own reasons.
- particularly to sk, a particular interest in scum/town reads, as cult doesn't want to convert someone that's gonna get hung/nkd

as d2 progresses we'll be able to search for more stuff that might distinguish sk/cult from each other, but as i'm taking the time during night to be more thorough in my thoughts and stuff when I can have guarantee nothing else will interrupt me, I have d1 to work with.
